questions.question: Rocky minor planet, mostly orbiting between Mars and Jupiter.
Numeral system that uses only two symbols, 0 and 1.
Electronic component that stores energy in an electric field.
Mythical reptilian creature often depicted hoarding treasure and breathing fire.
Thermodynamic measure of disorder or randomness in a system.
Force that opposes motion between surfaces in contact.
Attractive force between masses that gives weight to objects.
SI unit of frequency equal to one cycle per second.
Global network of networks that communicates using the TCP/IP protocol suite.
Common compressed image format standardized by the Joint Photographic Experts Group.
SI base unit for thermodynamic temperature.
Device that emits coherent, monochromatic light via stimulated emission.
Device that modulates and demodulates signals to connect to a network provider.
Nature spirit from Greek mythology, often associated with forests, rivers, or mountains.
Net movement of solvent through a semipermeable membrane toward higher solute concentration.
Quantum of electromagnetic radiation, carrying energy proportional to frequency.
Fundamental constituent of matter that comes in flavors such as up, down, and strange.
Networking device that forwards data packets between different networks.
Either time of year when the Sun reaches its maximum northern or southern declination.
Semiconductor device used to amplify or switch electronic signals.
Radioactive element with atomic number 92 used as nuclear reactor fuel.
Microscopic infectious agent that replicates only inside living cells.
Wireless networking technology based on IEEE 802.11 standards.
Noble gas used in high-intensity lamps and some ion thrusters.
Single-celled fungus used to ferment sugars in baking and brewing.
Metal essential for enzyme function and immunity, atomic number 30.
